channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: What the React Compiler Quietly Skips
item.description: React Compiler 1.0 went stable in October 2025. Half the tutorials I saw declared useMemo dead. It's not — and on most existing codebases, the compiler will silently skip the components you most...

item.title: Quick Guide: Connecting n8n to Any REST API in 10 Minutes
item.description: Connecting n8n to a REST API in under 10 minutes might sound ambitious, but it's quite achievable, even for beginners. I stumbled across n8n a few months ago when I was dealing with automating my...

item.title: Functional Emotions and Production Guardrails: What Interpretability Research Means for Claude Code
item.description: In April 2026, Anthropic published Emotion Concepts and their Function in a Large Language Model, a paper examining Claude Sonnet 4.5. Its central result is unusual and important: the model...

item.title: A jury is about to decide the fate of Ticketmaster
item.description: Consumer complaints about Ticketmaster are so voluminous at state attorneys general offices that Pennsylvania's comes with an explicit plea for residents lodging a grievance about the company to...

item.title: The Python Testing Toolkit: 4 Drop-In Files for Production pytest
item.description: Every Python project eventually needs the same test infrastructure. You write conftest.py from scratch, reach for the same Hypothesis patterns, copy async fixture code from a Stack Overflow answer...

item.title: VLOOKUP Doesn

item.title: Final 2 days to save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket
item.description: Ticket discounts of up to $500 will end tomorrow, April 10, at 11:59 p.m. PT. After that, prices for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 go up again. Miss this, and you’ll be paying more for the same access...

item.title: Enhancing Payment Fraud Detection with Rapyd Protect

item.title: IAsyncEnumerable Explained — Async Streams in .NET
item.description: IAsyncEnumerable, yield return, cancellation tokens, vs IEnumerable, real-world streaming Most developers know IEnumerable<T> and async/await. IEnumerable<T> — lets you iterate a sequence....

item.title: Sourcery vs Black: Refactoring vs Formatting
item.description: Quick Verdict Sourcery and Black are two of the most commonly mentioned Python code quality tools, but comparing them directly misses the point - they operate at entirely different layers of code...

item.title: Get Started in 1 Minute: Connect Memoria to OpenClaw
item.description: One command. Smarter memory. Cut token usage by 70%+. Why You Need This OpenClaw's built-in memory works — until it starts costing you. It loads everything, every time. OpenClaw's default memory...

item.title: The AI industry’s race for profits is now existential
item.description: Today on Decoder, let’s talk about the looming AI monetization cliff, and whether some of the biggest companies in the space can become real, profitable businesses before they careen right off it....

item.title: How Timestream Actually Bills: A Breakdown for Engineers
item.description: Timestream can look simple on the bill until you break down the line items. Most teams think in terms of "stored data," but Amazon Timestream for LiveAnalytics is billed across multiple meters...

item.title: The Pro-Iran Meme Machine Trolling Trump With AI Lego Cartoons
item.description: Since the beginning of the Iran war, the group Explosive Media has released over a dozen viral videos mocking Trump and the US.

item.title: Waymo is offering to help cities fix their potholes
item.description: In recent months, some cities have sought a new recruit in their forever war against potholes: Waymo. Municipal officials in multiple cities where Waymo operates have reached out to the robotaxi...

item.title: The Exit 8 movie is even better if you play the game first
item.description: There's an intriguing new video game adaptation to watch in theaters - and no, it's not that one. Exit 8, based on the game of the same name, hits the big screen on April 10th, and it's part of a...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Hybrid P2P Botnet, 13-Year-Old Apache RCE and 18 More Stories
item.description: Thursday. Another week, another batch of things that probably should've been caught sooner but weren't. This one's got some range — old vulnerabilities getting new life, a few "why was that even...

item.title: Amazon’s Starlink competitor Leo gets a new date
item.description: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy says the company's space-internet service Leo (formerly known as Project Kuiper) will "launch in mid-2026." I'm going to assume that means proper commercial availability...

item.title: The Download: AstroTurf wars and exponential AI growth
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Is fake grass a bad idea? The AstroTurf wars are far from...

item.title: The Hidden Security Risks of Shadow AI in Enterprises
item.description: As AI tools become more accessible, employees are adopting them without formal approval from IT and security teams. While these tools may boost productivity, automate tasks, or fill gaps in...

item.title: Adobe Reader Zero-Day Exploited via Malicious PDFs Since December 2025
item.description: Threat actors have been exploiting a previously unknown zero-day vulnerability in Adobe Reader using maliciously crafted PDF documents since at least December 2025. The finding, detailed by...

item.title: I tested three Windows laptops in the MacBook Neo’s price range — there’s no contest
item.description: When the MacBook Neo arrived last month, I knew Windows laptop makers were in trouble. For $599, the Neo offers fantastic build quality and solid performance in a sleek and ultra-portable package....

item.title: Google makes it easy to deepfake yourself
item.description: YouTube Shorts is rolling out a new AI-powered feature giving creators an easy way to realistically clone themselves on camera. The launch, hinted at earlier this year, reflects the platform's...

item.title: On Microsoft’s Lousy Cloud Security
item.description: ProPublica has a scoop: In late 2024, the federal government’s cybersecurity evaluators rendered a troubling verdict on one of Microsoft’s biggest cloud computing offerings. The tech giant’s “lack...

item.title: Bitter-Linked Hack-for-Hire Campaign Targets Journalists Across MENA Region
item.description: An apparent hack-for-hire campaign likely orchestrated by a threat actor with suspected ties to the Indian government targeted journalists, activists, and government officials across the Middle...

item.title: Spotify now lets you turn off all its videos
item.description: Spotify is adding new toggles to stop any and all video from playing inside the app, for both music and podcasts. The controls are rolling out worldwide, work across all platforms and devices, and...

item.title: Is fake grass a bad idea? The AstroTurf wars are far from over.
item.description: A rare warm spell in January melted enough snow to uncover Cornell University’s newest athletic field, built for field hockey. Months before, it was a meadow teeming with birds and bugs; now it’s...

item.title: Desalination technology, by the numbers
item.description: When I started digging into desalination technology for a new story, I couldn’t help but obsess over the numbers. I’d known on some level that desalination—pulling salt out of seawater to produce...

item.title: This AI Wearable From Ex-Apple Engineers Looks Like an iPod Shuffle
item.description: Two former Apple Vision Pro developers made an AI wearable that only listens when you tap it. They hope to win where other AI gadgets have fumbled: privacy.

item.title: Amflow’s latest e-bikes with Avinox motors raise the bar again
item.description: Amflow, the e-bike brand spun out of DJI, is back with two impressive electric mountain bike ranges built around its incredibly dense Avinox motors. The e-MTBs aim to break the traditional...
